samples.xml_description:
<module id="div_8bit">
    <description>A simplified radix-2 divider for 8-bit signed or unsigned integers.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">The clock signal for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst">The reset signal to initialize or reset the module.</input>
        <input id="sign">Indicates if the operation is signed (1) or unsigned (0).</input>
        <input id="dividend" width_description="[7:0]">The 8-bit input dividend for division.</input>
        <input id="divisor" width_description="[7:0]">The 8-bit input divisor for division.</input>
        <input id="opn_valid">A 1-bit signal indicating that a valid operation request is present.</input>
        <input id="res_ready">A 1-bit signal indicating that the 16-bit output result has already been acknowledged.</input>
        <output id="res_valid">A 1-bit output signal indicating the result is valid and ready.</output>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">The 16-bit output containing the remainder in the upper 8 bits and the quotient in the lower 8 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="IDLE">Idle state.</state>
        <state id="DIVIDE">Divide state.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="res_valid_reg" type="internal_signal">Register to hold the result validity status.</reg>
        <reg id="start_cnt" type="internal_signal">Register to indicate the start of the division process.</reg>
        <reg id="cnt" type="internal_signal">Register to keep track of the current iteration count.</reg>
        <reg id="neg_divisor" type="internal_signal">Register to store the negative value of the divisor.</reg>
        <reg id="remainder" type="internal_signal">Register to store the remainder during division.</reg>
        <reg id="quotient" type="internal_signal">Register to store the quotient during division.</reg>
        <reg id="SR" type="internal_signal">Shift Register to hold the dividend.</reg>
        <always_comb>
            // Combinational logic for result_valid
            if (rst == 1'b1 || cnt == 8'd8) begin
                res_valid = 1'b0;
            end else if ((res_valid & res_ready) == 1'b1) begin
                res_valid = 1'b0;
            end else begin
                res_valid = res_valid;
            end
        </always_comb>
        <always_ff @(posedge clk or posedge rst)> 
            // Sequential logic for res_valid_reg
            if (rst == 1'b1) begin
                res_valid_reg <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                res_valid_reg <= res_valid;
            end
        </always_ff>
        <always_ff @(posedge clk or posedge rst)> 
            // Sequential logic for start_cnt
            if (rst == 1'b1) begin
                start_cnt <= 1'b0;
            end else if (opn_valid == 1'b1 && res_valid != 1'b1) begin
                start_cnt <= 1'b1;
            end else if (cnt == 8'd8) begin
                start_cnt <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                start_cnt <= start_cnt;
            end
        </always_ff>
        <always_ff @(posedge clk or posedge rst)> 
            // Sequential logic for cnt
            if (rst == 1'b1) begin
                cnt <= 8'd0;
            end else if (start_cnt == 1'b1) begin
                cnt <= cnt + 1'b1;
            end else begin
                cnt <= 8'd0;
            end
        end
        <always_ff @(posedge clk or posedge rst)> 
            // Sequential logic for neg_divisor
            if (rst == 1'b1) begin
                neg_divisor <= 8'd0;
            end else if (start_cnt == 1'b1) begin
                neg_divisor <= ~divisor + 1'b1;
            end else begin
                neg_divisor <= neg_divisor;
            end
        </always_ff>
        <always_ff @(posedge clk or posedge rst)> 
            // Sequential logic for remainder
            if (rst == 1'b1) begin
                remainder <= 8'd0;
            end else if (start_cnt == 1'b1) begin
                remainder <= dividend[7];
            end else if (cnt > 8'd0) begin
                remainder <= remainder << 1;
                remainder[0] <= SR[7];
            end else begin
                remainder <= remainder;
            end
        </end
        <always_ff @(posedge clk or posedge rst)> 
            // Sequential logic for quotient
            if (rst == 1'b1) begin
                quotient <= 8'd0;
            end else if (start_cnt == 1'b1) begin
                quotient <= 8'd0;
            end else if (cnt > 8'd0) begin
                quotient <= quotient << 1;
                quotient[0] <= SR[7];
            end else begin
                quotient <= quotient;
            end
        </end
        <always_ff @(posedge clk or posedge rst)> 
            // Sequential logic for SR
            if (rst == 1'b1) begin
                SR <= 9'd0;
            end else if (start_cnt == 1'b1) begin
                SR <= {dividend, 1'b0};
            end else if (cnt > 8'd0) begin
                SR <= {remainder, SR[6:0]};
            end else begin
                SR <= SR;
            end
        </end
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        When opn_valid is high and res_valid is low, the module saves the inputs dividend and divisor.
        Initializes the shift register SR with the absolute value of the dividend shifted left by one bit.
        Sets NEG_DIVISOR to the negated absolute value of the divisor.
        Sets the counter cnt to 1 and start_cnt to 1 to begin the division process.
        Division Process(If start_cnt is high, the module performs the following steps):
        If the counter cnt reaches 8 (most significant bit of cnt is set), the division is complete:
        cnt and start_cnt are cleared.
        Updates the shift register SR with the final remainder and quotient.
        Otherwise, the counter cnt is incremented, and the shift register SR is updated based on the subtraction result:
        Computes the subtraction of NEG_DIVISOR.
        Uses a multiplexer to select the appropriate result based on the carry-out.
        Updates SR by shifting left and inserting the carry-out.
        Result Validity:
        res_valid is managed based on the reset signal, the counter, and whether the result has been consumed.
        res_valid should be update directly, without passing middle register value.
        res_valid is set to zero if rst is high.
        Otherwise, if cnt reaches 8, res_ready is set to 1.
        Otherwise, if res_valid and res_ready is all high, res_valid is set to 0.
        Otherwise, res_valid is set to res_valid.
    </implementation>
</module>
